resources.string: Brass teeth grind, oiled by the red tithe.

Peace is but the putrid gasp between hammer blows. Upon the ashen crust, brass hunger churns a river of gore. Legions of iron march the world flat. Great engines graft steel into marrow and flesh into metal. Pillars of soot rise until the crimson vault forgets the stars. No throne is high enough to command it. No soul is small enough to be spared. The war engine reaps them both.
The kiln of slaughter never cools. Its fires are stoked by the broken remains of the fallen, burning eternally to forge new weapons of carnage.

The world is a sepulcher of soot, strangled by palls of black smoke. Iron-shod legions march in an eternal tide, pulverizing the soil into dust. The great mechanism churns, oiled by the lifeblood of the fallen. There is no conquest, only the campaign. The cosmic gears demand a tribute of strife, and we are but the coal for the fire.
